prompt:
Develop a DispatchLearn Operations Settlement system for US-based on-demand field service organizations that must assign work, reconcile charges, and keep a verifiable offline record of workforce training and assessments. The service exposes resource-based APIs for identity and access (local username/password only), workforce profiles, training libraries (courses, reading assets, assessments/grades), service orders, dispatch assignments, and cashier settlement records. Integrations are supported through REST-style resources and LAN-only webhooks for user/course/grade synchronization and operational events such as learning completed, submission received, and scoring completed; event subscriptions must allow filtering by tenant, event type, and effective date range, and include a replay-safe delivery identifier. API authentication supports signed JWTs by default with optional OAuth2 for on-prem identity providers only; tokens expire after 30 minutes with rotating refresh tokens capped at 10 active sessions per user. Quota management enforces per-client limits (default 600 requests/minute, burst 120, and 10,000 webhook deliveries/day) with administrative overrides and auditability. Dispatch APIs support both grab-order and assigned modes with a single acceptance winner enforced by idempotency keys and database locking; orders auto-expire if not accepted within 15 minutes and auto-cancel if not started within a 2-hour scheduled window. Distance-based strategy must be offline-safe by using stored service zones, ZIP+4 centroids, or manually maintained distance tables (no map services), combined with workload caps (max 8 open tasks/agent) and reputation score (0–100) to rank recommendations.
 The backend uses Gin to structure API capability boundaries and request validation, GORM for transactional persistence, and MySQL as the system of record for all domain entities, running as a single Docker-deployed service without external network dependencies. Core tables include Users (unique username, salted password hash, role enum, last_login_at, failed_attempt_count), Roles/Permissions (scoped to API categories), Courses/ContentItems (type enum: epub/pdf/html, checksum, size_limit 50 MB), ReaderArtifacts (bookmarks/highlights/annotations with immutable history and export batches), Grades (numeric 0–100 with letter mapping, unique per user+assessment attempt), Orders (unique order_no, time_window_start/end, status enum, zone_id), DispatchAcceptances (unique order_id winner, idempotency_key unique), Payments (method enum: cash, check, card_present, house_account; multi-record per order), Invoices (status enum, tax fields, billing address), and Reports (parameter sets, generated_at, file pointer). Sensitive fields (grades, payment references, addresses) are encrypted at rest using AES-256 with key rotation every 180 days and masked in responses by role; all transport is TLS within the local environment. Security safeguards include anti-replay nonces for webhook signing, strict privilege escalation checks on role changes, tamper-evident audit logs for “who changed what and when” (retained 7 years), and traceable change logs for critical configurations (quotas, settlement rules). Backup and recovery must support nightly full backups plus 15-minute binlog retention, with a documented restore target of RPO 15 minutes and RTO 4 hours. Reporting and analytics provide time/region/category/channel KPIs (including recycling/returns volume, average order value, fulfillment timeliness, and exception rate), custom filters, and offline “scheduled delivery” by generating exports to a secured local drop folder with access controls and checksum verification.
